security_deposit_recovery.title: Recovering Security Deposit from Landlord

steps.action: Send a written notice to the landlord demanding return of deposit
steps.details: Send via registered post or courier with tracking. Keep a copy of the notice and delivery proof.
Attach the rent agreement, deposit receipt, CNIC copy, proof of vacating the property, and the written notice.

steps.action: File the complaint with the Rent Controller
steps.details: Visit the Rent Controller office in your district and submit the application with supporting documents.

required_documents.source: Issued by landlord at time of payment

required_documents.name_en: Written Notice to Landlord (with proof of delivery)
required_documents.source: Drafted by you or your lawyer

office.name: Rent Controller Office
office.note: Located in or near the District Courts complex of your city

security_deposit_recovery.relevant_law: West Pakistan Urban Rent Restriction Ordinance, 1959; Islamabad Rent Restriction Ordinance, 2001; Karachi Rent Restriction Act, 1953

tenant_eviction_response.title: Responding to an Eviction or Possession Notice

steps.action: Read the notice and record the deadline
steps.details: Check who issued the notice, the reason for eviction, rent arrears claimed, and any hearing date.
Gather the rent agreement, rent receipts, bank transfers, utility bills, and any messages with the landlord.

steps.action: File a reply before the Rent Controller or relevant court
steps.details: Submit your written reply before the deadline and attend all hearings with original documents.
steps.timeline: Before the date in the notice

required_documents.source: Receipts, bank, or mobile wallet record

office.name: Rent Controller Office
office.note: Attend the forum named in the notice or the district rent office

tenant_eviction_response.estimated_cost: PKR 500-3000 for basic filing and copying
tenant_eviction_response.relevant_law: Provincial rent restriction laws and the applicable rent ordinance for your city

property_registration.title: Registering a Property Sale or Transfer

steps.action: Prepare the sale deed or transfer deed
steps.details: Make sure names, CNIC numbers, property description, price, and boundaries match the title documents.

steps.action: Pay stamp duty and applicable government charges
steps.details: Use the valuation table and local revenue office guidance to calculate duty and taxes.

steps.action: Register the document with the Sub-Registrar
steps.details: Both parties or authorized representatives appear with witnesses, CNICs, photographs, and original documents.

required_documents.name_en: CNIC Copies of Buyer and Seller
required_documents.name_ur: خریدار اور فروخت کنندہ کے شناختی کارڈ

office.name: Sub-Registrar Office
office.note: Usually linked with the local revenue or district registration office

property_registration.estimated_cost: Depends on property value, stamp duty, registration fee, and local taxes
property_registration.estimated_timeline: 1 day to several weeks depending on document readiness
property_registration.relevant_law: Registration Act, 1908; Stamp Act, 1899; Transfer of Property Act, 1882
